channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Thousands flee as wildfires burn out of control in and around Los Angeles and homes are destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — California firefighters battled wind-whipped wildfires that tore across the Los Angeles area, destroying homes, clogging roadways as tens of thousands fled and straining resources as officials prepared for the situation to worsen overnight.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 5 in Gaza, including 2 babies, Palestinians say
item.description: Palestinian medics say Israeli airstrikes have killed at least five people in the Gaza Strip, including two infants and a woman. One strike hit a home in the central city of Deir al-Balah, killing two men and a woman, according to the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital, which received the casualties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Screen Actors Guild Awards cancel live nominations announcement due to Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Screen Actors Guild Awards nominations have canceled an in-person announcement planned for Wednesday morning due to devastating wildfires and winds in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CNN defamation trial comes at a rough time for legacy media
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At a particularly inopportune time for legacy media and CNN, the news outlet is on trial in Florida this week, accused of defaming a Navy veteran involved in rescuing endangered Afghans from that country when the U.S. ended its involvement there in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: The affluent Pacific Palisades neighborhood in Los Angeles has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawyers collecting $148 million judgment from Rudy Giuliani say World Series rings must be given up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for two former Georgia election workers who are owed $148 million in damages after suing Rudy Giuliani for defamation said Tuesday that evidence proves their clients are entitled to three World Series rings that the former New York City mayor says he gave to his son.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 death row inmates reject Biden's commutation of sentences
item.description: Shannon Agofsky and Len Davis believe the life sentences put them at a legal disadvantage with them planning to seek appeals based on claims of innocence.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: Sole survivor of West Valley murder-suicide expected to be released from hospital soon
item.description: The sole survivor of a murder-suicide in West Valley City that left five family members dead is expected to be released from the hospital soon.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials use bulldozer to shove abandoned vehicles out of the way near Los Angeles fire
item.description: Drivers who abandoned their vehicles fleeing the growing fire in Los Angeles might find their cars a little worse for wear when they return.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cybertruck driver's ex-girlfriend details his life before blast
item.description: Alicia Arritt spoke with NewsNation local affiliate KDVR about her former boyfriend, Matthew Livelsberger.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: New Orleans is a city marked by tragedy. But don’t call locals resilient
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — She ran around in silver sparkling shoes, her faux chainmail tunic shimmering in the freezing breeze, maneuvering horses made of paper mâché, a giant green dragon, and sheep constructed from milk cartons.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, SHARON LURYE, CLAIRE GALOFARO and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As the holiday honoring slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his teachings and work throughout the coming year, rather than just quoting him or celebrating for a day. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feds release new photo of New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar as FBI seeks more tips
item.description: FBI releases image showing New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar biking through the French Quarter on Halloween, months before attacking New Year's revelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Traffic has gotten way worse in these US cities, report finds
item.description: You're not imagining it. Traffic congestion worsened last year in just about every American city studied by navigation and GPS company TomTom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Wildfire breaks out in Pacific Palisades area of Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters scrambled to corral flames churning along Los Angeles hillsides as winds gained strength Tuesday across Southern California, where forecasters warned of “life-threatening, destructive” gusts that could last for days while toppling trees, creating dangerous surf and br
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: PALM BEACH, Florida (AP) — Less than two weeks before taking office, President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday took some of his most audacious claims and promises of the transition period and amped them up to new levels.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court proceedings for man charged in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing are delayed several weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a New York City hotel is unlikely to appear in Manhattan federal court again before mid-February after prosecutors and lawyers on Monday requested more time to prepare the case for trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: O'Malley touts Congressional Black Caucus members in DNC chair bid
item.description: “They are proven winners, and together, we will reengage, reconnect, and rebuild our party,” Former Maryland Gov. Martin O’Malley (D) said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Data company figures out which airlines are most on time
item.description: Mexican airline Aeromexico had the world's best record for on-time arrivals in 2024, according to an annual ranking released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — At a Mass celebrated Sunday at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, just blocks from where a man sped down Bourbon Street in a deadly attack early on New Year's Day, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains and asked for prayers for victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintry mix grips central US before moving east
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — Road conditions were deteriorating Saturday in the central U.S. as a winter storm brought a mix of snow, ice and plunging temperatures, with forecasts calling for the dreaded combo to spread eastward in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4
